title: Cyber-Sovereign Proof Benchmark Market
thesis: Cyber-sovereign work becomes an execution moat when improvements are benchmarked, independently reproduced, settled, and admitted into future context only after proof.
problem: A single cyber improvement is not enough. Serious buyers need benchmarks, task manifests, reproduction receipts, and challenge history.
mission: Create a proof benchmark market for authorized defensive cyber tasks: run candidate improvements, validate, red-team, reproduce independently, settle, and Chronicle.
why: This converts cyber proof from internal work into a market-readable execution moat.
goals: Turn cyber work into replayable proof
rsi: Only reproduced and validated benchmark improvements can become future operating context; every accepted benchmark improves the next benchmark market.
agialpha: AGIALPHA coordinates benchmark mission budgets, builder claims, reviewer/red-team bonds, evaluator incentives, proof-card actions, credential issuance, reward-vault settlement, and reputation-weighted routing.
